_readme: This is a template entry. To add a new certification, open certifications.json and append a new object following this schema. Delete all '_comment' keys from real entries.
_comment_id: Unique identifier. Convention: CERT-{PROVIDER_ABBR}-{CERT_ABBR} e.g. CERT-GCP-ACE, CERT-AWS-SAA, CERT-CKA
name: Full Certification Name
_comment_name: Exact official name as it appears on the certificate/badge.
_comment_provider: Issuing organization. Existing values: Google Cloud | Microsoft | GitHub | Coursera. Add new providers freely.
_comment_status: Options: ACTIVE | EXPIRED. EXPIRED certs are still shown but visually dimmed.
_comment_expires: ISO date format: YYYY-MM-DD, OR the string 'Lifetime' for non-expiring credentials.
_comment_credentialId: The credential / badge ID as shown on the certificate or Credly profile.
_comment_verifyUrl: Public verification URL. Use the Credly badge URL, Coursera verify URL, or the provider's own verification page.
_comment_image: Badge image URL. Prefer 340×340 Credly images. For non-Credly certs, use the provider's official badge PNG/SVG. Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.
_comment_color:
Tailwind text-color class applied to the provider icon / accent. Common choices:
    Google Cloud  → text-blue-500
    Microsoft     → text-blue-600
    AWS           → text-orange-500
    GitHub        → text-slate-100 (dark) or text-slate-800
    HashiCorp     → text-purple-500
    Red Hat       → text-red-500
    Linux Found.  → text-yellow-500
